title: Stictocardia lutambensis
intro: Stictocardia lutambensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae, commonly known as the "Kiburi" or "Ugali" plant, native to East Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Stictocardia lutambensis include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which have been isolated and studied for their medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often made into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, making it a valuable resource in folk medicine.
taxonomy: Stictocardia lutambensis is classified within the family Convolvulaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Stictocardia and is a member of the Malpighiales order. At the kingdom level, it falls under Plantae. Further classification places it in the phylum Angiospermophyta. The species is also identified as a flowering plant.
